channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Oil Spillage, 9th March
item.description:
Oil Spillage after road traffic collision
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 271, Chipperfield Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Request for Better Waste Solutions - Seven Sisters Road, 9th March
item.description:
Dear Council Team, Seven Sisters Road (near the park and commercial shops) is frequently covered with cardboard and litter left beside bins. This creates hygiene issues, blocks the pavement, and worsens during rain and wind. Could the council fix this. Ty

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Seven Sisters Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Poor Cleanliness especially on Match-Day Diversion Route - Finsbury Park Station, 9th March
item.description:
Dear Sir or Madam, I am writing to raise a concern regarding the cleanliness of the pathway at Finsbury Park Tube Station that is regularly used to divert Arsenal supporters on match days. This route is frequently used to manage large numbers of fans travelling to and from the stadium. Unfortunately, the area is often left in a very poor condition, with rubbish scattered across the floor and a particularly unpleasant amount of chewing gum covering the pavement. Given the heavy use of this pathway on match days, it would be beneficial if more regular cleaning and maintenance could be arranged. It may also be worth considering whether Arsenal Football Club could contribute to the upkeep of this route, as it is heavily used specifically for match-day crowd management. Improving the cleanliness of this pathway would make a significant difference to the local environment and to the experience of residents and visitors passing through the area. Thank you for taking the time to consider this matter. I hope steps can be taken to address the issue. Yours faithfully, L
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Place, Finsbury Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full / overflowing litter bin, 8th March
item.description:
Bin once again full and overflowing
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Avenue, Beckenham, Greater London

item.description:
Outside the estate agent, on the pillar
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, High Street, Beckenham, Greater London

item.title: Uncollected bin bags post street sweeping, 8th March
item.description:
Hi there, Please collect these bags asap, otherwise that defeats the work you do. Best, Lidia

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tunnel Avenue, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned shopping trolley under the bridge, 8th March
item.description:
Hi there, Please look after this area a bit more and do frequent checks, followed by cleaning. There is a fly-tipping spot next to the wall on the site if Alan's plants and shopping trolley abandoned under the bridge. Best, L

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tunnel Avenue, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping next to Alan's Plants & Angerstein Roundabout, 8th March
item.description:
Hi there, Please look after this area a bit more and do frequent checks, followed by cleaning. There is a fly-tipping spot next to the wall on the site if Alan's plants and shopping trolley abandoned under the bridge. Best, L

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tunnel Avenue, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Angerstein Roundabout full of litter, 8th March
item.description:
Hi there, This area meant to be cleaned twice a week but the current state does not confirm that at all - please, sweep the area asap. Best, L

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Horn Lane, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dishwasher(?) and other miscellaneous rubbish piled on top outside flat 7 Edgecombe House, 8th March
item.description:
This dishwasher has been left here for around a week and more rubbish is being left on top of it each day.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lettsom Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant flytipping, 8th March
item.description:
The businesses have locked bins but someone is dumping regularly . It looks disgusting and is attracting vermin

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 200, Union Street, Southwark, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: CO2 Canisters, 8th March
item.description:
Located gutter of Olyffe Avenue (south) on junction with Burnell Avenue. Box of canisters and several loose canisters.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burnell Avenue, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large potholes, 8th March
item.description:
Potholes in Glastonbury Rd N9

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Winchester Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter Nelson Mandela Road, 8th March
item.description:
Street has lots of litter, council should pick up in this area more. Nelson Mandela Road, lots of people throw rubbish out of cars
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nelson Mandela Road, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large crack and road surface collapse at kerb edge, 8th March
item.description:
The road surface has cracked and collapsed next to the kerb, leaving an open split in the asphalt and loose debris on the carriageway. This presents a safety risk for vehicles and cyclists and may quickly deteriorate into a larger pothole. Please arrange an inspection and repair as soon as possible.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Court Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Phone box at whitworth st, 8th March
item.description:
The poor red box at the end of the road has had a huge black sack full of dog poo dumped in it. It has been left there inside for a month now and it is awful to see walking past daily. It’s a shame, the phone box is only used by drug addicts to pee in. It is rusted and the phone never works. Maybe it’s time to remove it ? Or just lock it up?
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whitworth Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Council green litter bag left by bin, 8th March
item.description:
I requested that the bin at the end of Normanhurst Rd was empty because it was over flowing and the council worker took the bag out but left it by the bin and the foxes pulled it apart . I wondered if now this can be cleaned up and removed please. It’s at the Kelsey Rd end .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Normanhurst Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bed frame, 8th March
item.description:
There is a large bed frame as well as huge amounts of rubbish in this location.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Picardy Manorway, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
